title: Historian Initial Sweep — tracks
one_liner: Run the Historian advisor's initial_sweep mode to build baseline theme coverage and pattern index for this spoke.
why_this_matters: The initiative-theme tracking system requires each spoke's Historian to have a baseline sweep before theme imbalance patterns can fire accurately.

perceive.current_state: Historian advisor exists but has not run initial_sweep mode. Theme coverage patterns (theme_coverage_gap, theme_imbalance_detected) are installed but have no baseline data.
perceive.gap: Patterns will fire false positives until a sweep establishes baseline coverage.

steps: 1. Confirm WAI-Spoke/advisors/historian/v1.0.0.yaml exists and contains theme_coverage_gap and theme_imbalance_detected patterns. If missing, copy from framework spoke or note the gap.
2. Scan WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/epic/ recursively — collect all epic JSON files. For each: extract id, title, status, themes[] array.

3. Build theme coverage map for the 7 themes (quality, growth, impact, enablement, performance, awareness, satisfaction): list active (open/in_progress) epic IDs per theme. Flag themes with 0 active epics as neglected.

4. Write WAI-Spoke/advisors/historian/initial-sweep-results.json with structure: { swept_at, spoke, total_epics_scanned, active_epics, theme_coverage: { <theme>: { active_epics: [...], count: N, neglected: bool } }, neglected_themes: [...], epics_missing_themes: [...], baseline_established: true }
5. Validate the JSON is parseable with: python3 -c "import json; json.load(open('WAI-Spoke/advisors/historian/initial-sweep-results.json'))"
6. Move this lug file to WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/task/completed/task-historian-initial-sweep-tracks-v1.json with status: completed and completed_at timestamp.
7. Commit: git add WAI-Spoke/advisors/historian/initial-sweep-results.json WAI-Spoke/lugs/bytype/task/ && git commit -m 'chore(historian): initial theme sweep results'

done_when: All 7 themes are represented in theme_coverage

acceptance_criteria: initial-sweep-results.json written with theme coverage map
No false-positive theme_imbalance findings after sweep
